_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use short phrases when presenting an idea or showing something to our classmates.

_source.w: We are learning to use short phrases when presenting an idea or showing something to our classmates.
y: Presenting to others helps us build confidence in sharing our ideas.
Using phrases makes our sharing clearer and easier for others to understand.
Show-and-tell helps us practise being speakers in front of a group.
eg: We hold up something from home during show-and-tell and say a phrase about it.
We share a picture we drew and use short phrases to explain what it shows.
We tell classmates about a weekend activity using two or three words together.
We present a favourite toy and describe it using phrases like 'it is soft' and 'I like it'.
We use our device to say a short sentence about something we want to share.
We show an object to the class and tell them where we got it.
